## Highlights

Patch release on top of [0.5.2](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/tag/0.5.2).
Two reliability fixes for the concurrent `lvlab smoke` path and any concurrent
`virsh` use — no new features, no config changes.

- **`lvlab smoke` no longer trips over leftover VMs (#139).** A run that didn't
    finish teardown (Ctrl-C, crash, a batch dying mid-flight) could leave a
    same-named domain defined-but-off with a stale disk; the next run's
    `lvlab up` then *started that stale domain* instead of re-provisioning, so a
    static-IP case could come up on an old DHCP lease and fail verify on its
    configured address. Smoke now:
    - **reaps leftover case domains before the run** (it owns those names), so
        `up` always re-provisions cleanly;
    - **tears down in-flight VMs on an interrupted run** via a crash-safe
        `finally`, so a crash can't leave tomorrow's leftover; and
    - **self-diagnoses a static-vs-DHCP mismatch** — a static case that's
        unreachable while the guest holds a *different* lease now reports
        `static .194 unreachable; guest came up on .244 (DHCP fallback?)` instead
        of a bare `No route to host`.
- **`run_virsh` retries transient libvirtd connection drops (#129).** Under high
    concurrency the DBus-activated modular daemons (`virtqemud`) occasionally
    refuse a connection for a moment, failing a single `virsh` call with
    `failed to connect to the hypervisor` / `Remote peer disconnected` even
    though the request never reached the daemon. Those connection-class errors
    are now retried (3×, 0.5/1/2s backoff) instead of failing the whole command.
    Genuine command errors (bad args, missing domain), a missing binary, and
    timeouts still surface immediately.

## Breaking changes

None. Both changes are internal reliability fixes; behavior on success and on
genuine errors is unchanged.

## Install / upgrade

```bash
uv tool install --upgrade \
  https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/download/0.5.3/tkc_lvlab-0.5.3-py3-none-any.whl
```

## Supported hosts

Unchanged from 0.5.2 — Debian 12 (3.11), Debian 13 (3.13), AlmaLinux 10
(3.12), Fedora 44 (3.14). See
[`docs-extra/host-validation.md`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.5.3/docs-extra/host-validation.md)
for the matrix. The #139 leftover-VM guard, crash-safe teardown, and happy-path
lifecycle were confirmed end-to-end against real libvirt (`qemu:///system`).

## Also in this release (not user-facing)

- Docs: a cross-distro `runcmd` CA-trust example in
    `docs/cloud-init-examples.md` (Debian `update-ca-certificates` vs RHEL
    `update-ca-trust`).
- Repo hygiene: removed a stray `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` that had been
    committed by accident.
